text.title: Rust Testing Antipatterns
text.summary: Common failures: unwrap() in SUT, mock ordering bugs, async drops, snapshot abuse, and env var collisions.

antipattern.description: `unwrap()` called inside the SUT code path under test (not in the test body).
antipattern.reason: `unwrap()` in tests is fine — it's the expected failure mode for test assertions. `unwrap()` in the SUT means the code panics on errors instead of returning Result. Use `?` in production code.

antipattern.description: mockall `.times(1)` placed before `.returning(...)` in the wrong order.
antipattern.reason: The order of mockall expectation builder calls matters. `.times(1)` after `.returning(...)` specifies the call count. Placed before, it has different semantics. Agents copy from old examples and produce silent under/over-call errors.

antipattern.description: `tokio::spawn` called in a test without awaiting the returned handle.
antipattern.reason: The test runtime exits before the spawned task completes; assertions inside the task never run. Always await `JoinHandle` or use `JoinSet`.

antipattern.description: Running `cargo insta accept` in CI to auto-accept all snapshots.
antipattern.reason: Auto-accepting snapshots locks in any regression as the "expected" value. `cargo insta review` must be run locally; CI should reject unapproved snapshot changes.

antipattern.description: `std::env::set_var` called inside a test without a serial mutex.
antipattern.reason: Environment variable mutations are process-wide. Parallel test threads calling `set_var` for the same key collide, causing non-deterministic failures. Use `serial_test::serial` or refactor the SUT to accept config via DI.

antipattern.description: `tokio::time::sleep` in tests using real wall clock time.
antipattern.reason: Real sleep is flaky on loaded CI runners. Use `tokio::time::pause()` + `tokio::time::advance(Duration)` for deterministic time control in async tests.
